fact: October 28 is exactly 303 days into the year, which means there are only 62 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for squirrels to go absolutely nuts burying acorns - they can bury up to 10,000 acorns in a single season!
The sun sets before 6 PM in most northern cities by this date, making it feel like bedtime when you're still doing homework.
October 28 falls smack in the middle of Scorpio season - the zodiac sign represented by a creature that glows under black light!
Maple trees in cold regions are dropping their leaves like crazy right now, creating natural confetti storms in your backyard.
Why do spiders seem to be everywhere indoors around this time? They're actually seeking warm hiding spots for winter - your house is their cozy hotel!
The morning frost starts appearing on grass in many temperate regions, creating millions of tiny ice crystals that look like nature's glitter.
October 28 is roughly 35 days past the autumn equinox, meaning daylight is shrinking by about 2-3 minutes every single day in northern areas.
Monarch butterflies are in full migration mode, with some flying over 50 miles per day - that's like a tiny insect running two marathons!
In the Southern Hemisphere, October 28 means spring is in full swing and jacaranda trees are exploding with purple flowers that carpet entire streets.
Bears in northern regions are in hyperphagia mode right now - they're eating up to 20,000 calories per day, which is like eating 40 hamburgers!
